name: Suspicious RDP Redirect Using TSCON Detected
description: Detects a suspicious RDP session redirect using `tscon.exe`. Adversaries may use `tscon.exe` to connect to a different session, potentially hijacking an existing RDP session to maintain access, bypass authentication, and facilitate lateral movement.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: the process **{image}** executed a command line containing the pattern `*/dest:rdp-tcp:*`, which indicates a suspicious **RDP session redirect**. This is a strong indicator of **Lateral Movement** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1021.001**) where an adversary is likely attempting to hijack an RDP session to gain unauthorized access to another user's session or to escalate privileges. The command line was **{command}** and the event occurred under the user **{user}**. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action and to remove the malicious process.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The process image that performed the action.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line that was executed.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the action.
